name: Türkiye-Syria Earthquake Relief Corridors 2023
description: A disaster-response notebook for the February 2023 earthquakes, linking impacted urban zones, damaged infrastructure, cross-border aid routes, and the transition from rescue to relief.

sides.name: Türkiye response architecture
sides.description: Türkiye's state and provincial response operated across a huge damage belt while also serving as the rear logistics platform for cross-border aid into Syria.

subUnits.name: AFAD and provincial response
subUnits.description:
Türkiye's disaster management authority coordinated search, shelter, and logistics across multiple heavily affected provinces.
- Role: National disaster coordination
- Geographic anchor: Centered on Gaziantep, Kahramanmaraş, Hatay, and the wider southern provincial network.
- Why it matters: It linked local rescue pressure to national logistics, shelter, and cross-border coordination.
- What to watch: Road access, shelter demand, fuel, and how quickly provincial nodes regained operational rhythm.

subUnits.name: WHO and WFP logistics nodes
subUnits.description:
International logistics and health actors helped move aid through Turkish hubs and toward the most stressed areas.
- Role: Relief routing and health support
- Geographic anchor: Linked airports, warehouses, and onward road corridors in southern Türkiye to border points serving northwest Syria.
- Why it matters: Their performance determined how quickly the response could shift from rescue shock to sustained relief.
- What to watch: Aid throughput, health facility support, and corridor reliability.

sides.name: Cross-border Syria response
sides.description: Northwest Syria depended on local responders and cross-border channels that were already politically constrained before the earthquakes.

subUnits.name: Syrian Civil Defence and local responders
subUnits.description:
Local search-and-rescue and community responders were among the first actors operating inside devastated Syrian communities.
- Role: Immediate local rescue and relief
- Geographic anchor: Centered in northwest Syrian towns and settlements west and south of the Turkish border crossings.
- Why it matters: They bridged the gap before larger aid volumes could move across the border.
- What to watch: Rescue access, heavy equipment shortages, and speed of relief handoff.

subUnits.name: UN cross-border relief channel
subUnits.description:
The UN cross-border mechanism was essential for moving international aid from Türkiye into northwest Syria.
- Role: Cross-border corridor management
- Geographic anchor: Focused on Bab al-Hawa first, and then on additional authorized crossings as access widened.
- Why it matters: Without these routes, stockpiles in Türkiye could not become timely relief inside Syria.
- What to watch: Crossing status, customs and permissions, convoy scheduling, and onward distribution inside Syria.

events.title: Main shock strikes southern Türkiye
events.description: The first major earthquake devastated a wide belt of provinces in Türkiye and affected communities across the border in Syria, immediately overwhelming local response capacity.

events.title: Second major quake compounds the emergency
events.description: A second major earthquake struck hours later, widening the impact zone, damaging already stressed routes, and increasing the need for shelter and medical support.

events.title: Roads, airports, and depots struggle to stabilize
events.description: The response shifted from pure rescue urgency to a logistics problem as roads, fuel, hospitals, and airport operations all showed varying levels of disruption.

events.title: Bab al-Hawa becomes the central cross-border relief hinge
events.description: For northwest Syria, the crossing became the key geographic question: whether enough aid could move across fast enough to meet the spike in demand.

events.title: Additional crossings are authorized
events.description: The opening of extra border points widened the map of possible relief movement and reduced dependence on a single corridor into northwest Syria.

events.title: Response shifts toward sustained shelter and health support
events.description: As the rescue phase receded, the geography of the operation increasingly centered on tents, temporary shelter, damaged hospitals, and reliable onward distribution.

meta.name: Main quake impact belt
meta.description: An illustrative impact belt covering the most heavily affected urban and peri-urban areas in southern Türkiye and adjacent Syria.

meta.name: Gaziantep logistics hub
meta.description: Major Turkish urban node and logistics center relevant for relief staging, warehousing, and onward movement toward the border and quake zone.

meta.name: Bab al-Hawa crossing
meta.description: The principal cross-border point for aid entering northwest Syria from Türkiye at the start of the emergency.

meta.name: İskenderun port area
meta.description: Important logistics zone near the Mediterranean coast that also suffered major disruption in the aftermath of the quakes.

meta.name: Türkiye to northwest Syria relief corridor
meta.description: Simplified line showing the corridor from Turkish logistics hubs to the main cross-border entry and onward toward northwest Syrian communities.
